summary: Comprehensive backend testing for Salt Check MVP via public ingress URL. 31/31 pytest cases passed (100%). All CRUD endpoints (users, daily-entries, projects, tasks, money-entries, body-logs, person-notes) function correctly with proper data persistence and 404 handling. PEPPER AI check-in successfully calls Emergent LLM (GPT-4.1) and returns structured JSON (quick_read, salt_check, parked, money_check, body_check, next_sane_step, closer). Crisis-keyword detection ('want to die') correctly bypasses the LLM and returns the hard-coded safe response with no sass and no 'park' language. PEPPER history endpoint returns past check-ins in created_at desc order. Backend MongoDB persistence verified via Create -> GET round-trips.

minor.issue: Root route is not exposed via Kubernetes ingress (only /api/* is routed). Not blocking - /api/users and other /api endpoints reachable. Consider mounting health under /api/health for prod readiness probes.
Uses Mongo ObjectId() casting on raw path param; passing a non-ObjectId string raises bson.errors.InvalidId and returns 500 instead of 404/400. Add try/except and return 404.
Same ObjectId casting risk - invalid id strings will 500 instead of 404.

JSON parsing of LLM output uses bare 'except:' and silently falls back to a generic response. Sometimes the LLM wraps JSON in ```json fences which would also fail. Suggest stripping fences before json.loads and logging the parse error.
Returns 200 instead of 201 on creation (minor REST style).
DB_NAME in backend/.env is 'test_database' - rename to 'saltcheck_db' (or env-specific) before production seeding.

action_items: Wrap ObjectId() calls in all CRUD endpoints with InvalidId handling so malformed IDs return 404 instead of 500.
Strip ```json fences and log parse failures in pepper_checkin instead of bare except.
Consider adding /api/health (or moving root to /api/) so the public ingress has a reachable health endpoint.
Tighten 'detect_crisis' (currently substring matches like 'suicide' inside 'suicide-prevention-hotline' would trigger crisis path). Consider word-boundary regex.
Add response_model_exclude={'_id'} or explicit projection - currently _id is excluded only because Pydantic Response model omits it; safe today but fragile.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 654 lines - approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting routers per resource.
Heavy DRY violation: every CRUD endpoint repeats the same create/update/get/delete boilerplate. Extract a generic Mongo CRUD helper.
user_id is taken as a query parameter with default 'default_user' - acceptable for MVP, but flag as auth debt.
POST endpoints return 200 instead of the more conventional 201.
Daily entry POST does not enforce uniqueness on (user_id, date) - duplicate entries for the same day are allowed.
EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is loaded but no startup check; if missing, PEPPER fails at request-time with 500. Validate at startup.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed users, daily entries, projects, tasks, money entries, body logs, person notes, and PEPPER check-ins under isolated user_ids (TEST_*_user). Projects/tasks/notes are cleaned up on teardown. Daily entries, money entries, body logs, PEPPER checkins, and users are left behind in MongoDB (no DELETE endpoint exposed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL routes only /api/* through ingress. Use it via conftest. All 31 backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_saltcheck_backend.py currently green. PEPPER LLM call uses GPT-4.1 via emergentintegrations - real API call costs tokens; tests average ~5s total. Crisis path is keyword-based (no LLM) and instant.
rca_of_issue: No bugs reproduced. All requested features (1-10) verified working. Crisis detection verified by sending 'I want to die' which routes to the hard-coded safe response (no LLM call) and contains 'emergency'/'crisis' language and excludes the word 'park'.
